commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed because automation can identify the code defects, but cannot supply funded live lifecycle proof or accept the deferred attestation boundary.; Needs attention: The new SSH-over-TLS proxy should explicitly fail closed on certificate and hostname verification before this provider path merges.; Review finding: [P1] Persist successful non-repo Phala claims â€” internal/pro... (sha=abffc3931442a898503f7466ee8674055d6d3f09)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Linux-only Phala Cloud/dstack confidential TDX SSH-lease provider with CLI config, proxying, provider registration, docs, tests, and live-smoke support.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects by source inspection: the successful acquire path calls the repo claim helper with an empty repo root, whose implementation returns without writing a claim, and the proxy argv lacks fail-closed verification flags. The central provider feature still needs live Phala validation rather than a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed because automation can identify the code defects, but cannot supply funded live lifecycle proof or accept the deferred attestation boundary.

target.title: feat(providers): add Phala confidential TDX CVM SSH-lease provider
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `phala` as a Linux-only `ProviderKindSSHLease` instance provider, adapted
faithfully from `internal/providers/namespaceinstance`. It leases a Phala Cloud /
dstack **confidential Intel TDX CVM** via the `phala` CLI, SSHes in over crabbox's
normal sync/run path, and releases it. This is the **first confidential / attested
execution substrate** in crabbox â€” every existing instance provider leases a plain
VM; a Phala box runs inside a hardware TDX enclave.

## What's in this PR

- `internal/providers/phala/{provider,flags,backend,backend_test}.go` â€” full
  lease/run/release lifecycle (Acquire/Resolve/List/Doctor/ReleaseLease/Touch/
  Cleanup), structurally mirroring `namespaceinstance`.
- `internal/cli/phala_proxy.go` â€” `__phala-proxy` SSH gateway shim.
- `internal/cli/config.go` â€” `PhalaConfig` + file/env wiring; `claim.go`/`app.go`/
  `providers/all/all.go` â€” registration + node-scoped claims + dispatch.
- `docs/providers/phala.md` + regenerated provider matrix; `scripts/live-phala-smoke.sh`.

CLI mapping (verified against the live authenticated `phala` v1.1.19 CLI):
`deploy --json --dev-os --ssh-pubkey <pub> --wait [-n crabbox-<lease>] [-t tdx.small]`,
`cvms list/get/delete`. **No API key is ever passed as a flag** (stored auth only).

## Scope / non-goals (honest)

- **In:** lease / run / release, one focused concern.
- **Deferred:** TDX attestation (`cvms attestation`) â€” intentionally **not** advertised,
  because crabbox has no confidential/attestation `Feature` constant yet. Natural follow-up.
- Direct-only (no coordinator/broker); Linux-only; only `tdx.small` exercised.

## Verification

- `go build ./...`, `go vet ./internal/providers/phala/... ./internal/cli/...`, and
  the `phala` + `cli -run Phala` test suites all pass locally (rebased on current `main`).
- Structural parity with the merged Namespace Compute provider (#305), including its
  per-lease SSH key, claim-and-destroy, and ownership tests.

## Live proof â€” honest status

The guarded live smoke (`scripts/live-phala-smoke.sh`) is **`environment_blocked`**: it
requires a funded Phala account, which I don't have, so the full leaseâ†’runâ†’release has
**not** been exercised against live Phala hardware. What *is* validated: the `phala` CLI
command mapping against the real CLI, and â€” separately â€” that an Intel TDX CVM produces a
genuine hardware-rooted TDX v4 attestation quote (validated on real TDX silicon). The
end-to-end live smoke is ready to run the moment the account is funded.

## Review

The crabbox-native `autoreview` skill wouldn't run in my (Windows) environment, so I ran a
Claude adversarial review across three lenses (adaptation correctness, security, config
wiring). It found and I fixed: (1) an **ownership-safety drift** â€” since Phala exposes no
server-side labels, `List`/`Cleanup`/`ReleaseLease` now require a matching **local crabbox
claim**, so a foreign `crabbox-*`-named CVM is never listed/cleaned/released (regression
tests added); (2) a **flag bug** â€” `cvms list` takes no node flag, so the stray `--node`
was removed. Security review found no exploitable issues (no credential in argv, injection
contained, per-lease keys correct).

## Known limitation / risk

`phala_proxy.go` resolves the SSH gateway host/port from `cvms get` JSON using field names
derived from docs (not yet observed on a populated live payload) â€” flagged with an in-code
`TODO(phala)`. Parsing is fully defensive (no panic, clear error on miss). This is the one
thing the funded live smoke will pin down.

## AI assistance

Implemented with substantial Claude assistance (adaptation from the template, review, and
fixes); CLI mapping verified against the live CLI by a human-directed session.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
